words.def: to leave your job and stop working, especially because you have reached a particular age or because you are ill; to tell somebody they must stop doing their job
words.example: He is retiring next year after 30 years with the company.

words.def: having retired from work
words.example: a retired doctor/teacher/officer/general

words.def: to change your opinions or plans, for example because of something you have learned
words.example: I can see I will have to revise my opinions of his abilities now.

words.def: an increase in an amount, a number or a level
words.example: The industry is feeling the effects of recent price rises.

words.def: the possibility of something bad happening at some time in the future; a situation that could be dangerous or have a bad result
words.example: The health risks are very low.

words.def: a machine that can perform a complicated series of tasks by itself
words.example: These cars are built by robots.

words.def: a small loaf of bread for one person
words.example: Soup and a roll: £3.50

words.def: connected with or about love or a sexual relationship
words.example: a romantic candlelit dinner

words.def: very strong thick string made by twisting thinner strings, wires, etc. together
words.example: The rope broke and she fell 50 metres onto the rocks.

words.def: having a surface that is not even or regular
words.example: rough ground

words.def: connected with or belonging to the king or queen of a country
words.example: the royal family

words.def: things that you throw away because you no longer want or need them
words.example: a rubbish bag/bin

words.def: a game played by two teams of 13 or 15 players, using an oval ball which may be kicked or carried. Teams try to put the ball over the other team’s line.
words.example: to play a game of rugby

words.def: a statement of what may, must or must not be done in a particular situation or when playing a game
words.example: She laid down strict rules for her tenants, including prompt payment of rent.

words.def: the state of being safe and protected from danger or harm
words.example: in safety, a place where children can play in safety

words.def: a person who works on a ship as a member of the crew
words.example: a crew of two officers and 13 sailors

words.def: a substance that consists of very small fine grains of rock. Sand is found on beaches, in deserts, etc.
words.example: a grain of sand

words.def: to look quickly but not very carefully at a document, etc.
words.example: scan something for something, I scanned the list quickly for my name.

words.def: frightened of something or afraid that something bad might happen
words.example: The thieves got scared and ran away.

words.def: frightening
words.example: It was a really scary moment.

words.def: involving science; connected with science
words.example: a scientific discovery/theory/fact

words.def: the number of points, goals, etc. scored by each player or team in a game or competition
words.example: a high/low score

words.def: a written text of a play, film, broadcast, talk, etc.
words.example: That line isn't in the original script.

words.def: a work of art that is a solid figure or object made by carving or shaping wood, stone, clay, metal, etc.
words.example: a marble sculpture of Venus

words.def: any of the four main periods of the year: spring, summer, autumn and winter
words.example: the changing seasons

words.def: known about by only a few people; kept hidden from others
words.example: secret information/meetings/talks

words.def: the activities involved in protecting a country, building or person against attack, danger, etc.
words.example: national/homeland security (= the defence of a country)

words.def: the small hard part produced by a plant, from which a new plant can grow
words.example: a packet of wild flower seeds

words.def: forming a unit by itself; not joined to something else
words.example: They have begun to sleep in separate rooms.

words.def: a set of television or radio programmes or podcasts that deal with the same subject or that have the same characters
words.example: The first episode of the new series is on Saturday.

words.def: bad or dangerous
words.example: a serious illness/offence/crime

words.def: in a serious way
words.example: to be seriously ill/injured/wounded/hurt

words.def: a system that provides something that the public needs, organized by the government or a private company
words.example: the ambulance/bus/postal service

words.def: a group of similar things that belong together in some way
words.example: a set of six chairs

words.def: an environment where something is located; the place at which something happens
words.example: a rural/an ideal/a beautiful/an idyllic setting

words.def: the state of being male or female
words.example: How can you tell what sex a fish is?

words.def: an act of shaking somebody/something
words.example: She gave him a shake to wake him.

words.def: one part of something that is divided between two or more people
words.example: share of something, Next year we hope to have a greater share of the market.

words.def: having a fine edge or point, especially of something that can cut or make a hole in something
words.example: a sharp knife

words.def: a large piece of thin cloth used on a bed to lie on or lie under
words.example: Have you changed the sheets (= put clean sheets on the bed)?

words.def: a flat board, made of wood, metal, glass, etc., fixed to the wall or forming part of a cupboard, bookcase, etc., for things to be placed on
words.example: I helped him put up some shelves in his bedroom.

words.def: the hard outer part of eggs, nuts, some seeds, and some animals
words.example: We collected shells on the beach.

words.def: to produce or reflect light; to be bright
words.example: The sun shone brightly in a cloudless sky.

words.def: smooth and bright; reflecting the light
words.example: shiny black hair

words.def: not open
words.example: The door was shut.

words.def: nervous or embarrassed about meeting and speaking to other people
words.example: He is not exactly the shy and retiring type.

words.def: the ability to see
words.example: to lose your sight (= to become blind)

words.def: a movement or sound that you make to give somebody information, instructions, a warning, etc.
words.example: a danger/warning/distress signal

words.def: where there is little or no sound; making little or no sound
words.example: At last the traffic fell silent.

words.def: showing a lack of thought, understanding, or judgement
words.example: a silly idea/question/name

words.def: the state of being similar to somebody/something but not exactly the same
words.example: similarity between A and B, The report highlights the similarity between the two groups.

words.def: in almost the same way
words.example: It is a little cheaper than other similarly sized cars.

words.def: used to emphasize how easy or basic something is
words.example: To order, simply click here.

words.def: to go down below the surface or towards the bottom of a liquid or soft substance
words.example: The ship sank to the bottom of the sea.

words.def: the layer of tissue that covers the body
words.example: to have dark/pale skin

words.def: the natural state of rest in which your eyes are closed, your body is not active, and your mind is not conscious
words.example: I need to get some sleep.

words.def: a thin flat piece of bread, meat, cheese, etc. that has been cut off a larger piece; a piece of cake that has been cut from a larger cake
words.example: Cut the meat into thin slices.

words.def: a little
words.example: a slightly different version

words.def: not moving, acting or done quickly; taking a long time; not fast
words.example: a slow pace/speed

words.def: looking clean and neat; well dressed in fashionable and/or formal clothes
words.example: I have to be smart for work.

words.def: completely flat and even, without any rough areas or holes
words.example: the smooth surface of the metal

words.def: the programs used by a computer for doing particular jobs
words.example: management/editing software

words.def: hard; not in the form of a liquid or gas
words.example: The planet Jupiter may have no solid surface at all.

words.def: occasionally rather than all of the time
words.example: Sometimes I go by car.
